```yaml
name: kenya_chw_swahili
countries:
  primary:
    - Kenya
  extended:
    - Tanzania
    - Uganda
languages:
  - name: Swahili
    code: sw
    notes: Primary language of community health workers.
  - name: English
    code: en
    notes: Secondary / administrative language.
  - name: Sheng
    notes: Urban slang blend; common in peri-urban deployments.
writing_systems:
  - Latin
literacy_rates:
  - country: Kenya
    rate: "82%"
cultural_norms_notes: >
  Oral communication dominates; trusted intermediaries mediate formal health
  messaging.
infrastructure_notes: >
  Low-bandwidth mobile (2G/3G); voice-to-text is common.
domain_specific_notes: >
  CHWs operate under Kenyan MoH triage protocols.
web_search_enrichment:
  searches_performed:
    - query: "Kenya community health worker mobile deployment 2024"
      key_findings: "CHW programs use SMS and USSD as primary interfaces."
    - query: "Swahili medical NLP benchmarks"
      key_findings: "Limited availability; KenSwQuAD is most common reference."
```
